title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Runtime Lens: Input Accessibility
summary: The ship fires every frame a key is held, misses quit events, keeps thrust after focus loss, hard-codes WASD, assumes one controller identity, and requires precise simultaneous presses. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the input accessibility increment in device-independent remappable action layer. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.
Release latched actions and degrade safely when window focus, controller instance, battery, or audio device changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 1350 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause. A defensible solution applies Input Accessibility, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to device-independent remappable action layer.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-input-events-remapping-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-input-events-remapping-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_input_events_remapping_2(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Input Accessibility evidence · Theory Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-input-accessibility-1p1z1s" that uses case 8931 to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-input-accessibility-1p1z1s
# Competency: asteroids-input-accessibility.
# Case 8931: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_input_accessibility_1p1z1s(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1p1z1s = "1p1z1s-s1z1p1-8931"
    assert evidence_variant_1p1z1s
    evidence_axes_1p1z1s = (
        "player1p1z1s",
        "seed8931",
        "inputs1z1p1",
        "frame1p1z1s8931",
        "state89311p1z1s",
        "playtests1z1p18931",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1p1z1s)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

options.text: For repair the input accessibility defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Input Accessibility, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the input accessibility defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Accessibility model · Theory Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Input Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8456 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Accessibility model · Theory Input Accessibility.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Accessibility model · Theory Input Accessibility, keep Input Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Accessibility model · Theory Input Accessibility, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Input Accessibility precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Accessibility model · Theory Input Accessibility, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 8456 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Accessibility model · Theory Input Accessibility, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Adding controller support automatically makes input accessible.
